popularStory.headline: BIO
popularStory.description:
BIO:  Duke of Bavaria, 1120.  Became a lay brother of his foundation at Weingarten, and was buried there.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/BAVARIA.htm#HeinrichIXBavariadied1126B as of 8/7/2016 …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen於1075年出生在可特格希 貝也時，他的父親Herzog Welf von Bayern I38歲，母親Judith de Flandre42歲。他於從大約1095年到1100年與Wulfhilde von Sachsen在德國薩辛結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1126年12月13日在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 拉文斯包格拉文斯包格享年51歲，埋葬在巴登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen于1075年出生在可特格希 貝也时，他的父亲Herzog Welf von Bayern I38岁，母亲Judith de Flandre42岁。他于从大约1095年到1100年与Wulfhilde von Sachsen在德国薩辛结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1126年12月13日在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 拉文斯包格拉文斯包格享年51岁，埋葬在巴登。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a pet form of   Bayer  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power’. See Henry .

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: IX.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: probably a contracted form of  Jep , vernacular form of   Jacob   .

en.lifeSummary: When Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen was born in 1075, in Bavaria, his father, Herzog Welf von Bayern I, was 38 and his mother, Judith de Flandre, was 42. He married Wulfhilde von Sachsen from about 1095 to 1100, in Saxony, Germany. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 13 December 1126, in Ravensburg, Ravensburg, Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 51, and was buried in Baden.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen est né en 1075, à Königreich Bayern, son père, Herzog Welf von Bayern I, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Judith de Flandre, avait 42 ans. Il a épousé Wulfhilde von Sachsen du environ 1095 au 1100, à Sachsen, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 13 décembre 1126, à Ravensburg, Oberamt Ravensburg, Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterré à Baden.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen 1075 in Königreich Bayern geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herzog Welf von Bayern I 38 und seine Mutter Judith de Flandre 42. Er hat von um 1095 bis 1100 in Sachsen, Deutschland Wulfhilde von Sachsen geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 13. Dezember 1126 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Ravensburg, Oberamt Ravensburg, Württemberg, Deutschland und wurde in Baden begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen è nato il nel 1075 nel Königreich Bayern, il suo padre, Herzog Welf von Bayern I, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Judith de Flandre, aveva 42 anni. Lui sposato Wulfhilde von Sachsen da circa 1095 a 1100, nel Sachsen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 13 dicembre 1126, nel Ravensburg, Oberamt Ravensburg, Württemberg, Germania, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolto a Baden.

ja.lifeSummary: Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzenが1075年にクoニグレイチ バイエルンで生まれた時，父Herzog Welf von Bayern Iは38歳，母Judith de Flandreは42歳であった。から約1095年まで1100年にドイツサチセンでWulfhilde von Sachsenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1126年12月13日にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ラベンスブルグラベンスブルグで51歳で死去し，バデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen(이)가 1075년 ko니그레이치 베이른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herzog Welf von Bayern I(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith de Flandre(은)는 42세였다. 그는 대략 1095년 부터 1100년 까지 독일 작센에서 Wulfhilde von Sachsen(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1126년 12월 13일 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 레이븐스버그 레이븐스버그에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 바든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen nasceu em 1075, em Königreich Bayern, seu pai, Herzog Welf von Bayern I, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Judith de Flandre, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Wulfhilde von Sachsen de aproximadamente 1095 a 1100, em Sachsen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de dezembro de 1126, em Ravensburg, Oberamt Ravensburg, Württemberg, Alemanha, com 51 anos, e foi sepultado em Baden.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen родился в 1075 г. в местности кoниграич беиирн, его отцу по имени Herzog Welf von Bayern I было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Judith de Flandre, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Wulfhilde von Sachsen с около 1095 по 1100 в местности сачсен, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 13 декабря 1126 г. в местности равенсбург, обирамт равенсбург, вuрттембирг, Германия в возрасте 51 год и был похоронен в местности баден.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heinrich IX. Herzog von Bayern dem Schwarzen nació en 1075, en Königreich Bayern, su papá, Herzog Welf von Bayern I, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Judith de Flandre, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Wulfhilde von Sachsen desde aproximadamente 1095 hasta 1100, en Sachsen, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 13 de diciembre de 1126, en Ravensburg, Oberamt Ravensburg, Württemberg, Alemania, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultado en Baden.
